bio.education: Santa Barbara High School (Santa Barbara, Calif.) UCLA
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Played in his first beach volleyball tournament at 11 years old
  • Earned his AAA rating in beach volleyball at age 17
  • Joined the U.S. Men’s National Indoor Volleyball Team in 1981
  • Attended UCLA where he won three NCAA championships under head coach Al Scates
  • Was the youngest player on the 1984 U.S. Olympic gold medal indoor volleyball team
  • Was named the top volleyball player in the world by the Federation Internationale de Volleyball (FIVB) in 1986 and 1988
  • Won at least one tournament in 24 out of the 28 season he played beach volleyball professionally
  • Claimed titles with 13 different partners in beach volleyball
  • First began coaching at St. Margaret's Episcopal High School
  • Served as an assistant coach of the 2012 U.S. Olympic Women’s National Indoor Volleyball Team
  • Named head coach of the 2012 U.S. Women’s National Indoor Volleyball Team
  • First person to win a world championship gold medal as both a player and a coach
  • Honored by the FIVB as the greatest volleyball player of the 20th century
  • Has two children, Kristian and Kory.
Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian; 3-time Olympic medalist (3 gold)
    • Olympic Games Atlanta 1996, gold (Team - Men)
    • Olympic Games Seoul 1988, gold (Team - Men)
    • Olympic Games Los Angeles 1984, gold (Team - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 1999 – 5th (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 1986, 1987, 1988, 1997, 1999
  • Medals: 3 (2 gold, 1 silver)
    • Gold – 1988 (Team - Men); 1986 (Team - Men)
    • Silver – 1987 (Team - Men)

  • Speciality: 500m, Team Sprint

  • Played hockey until 2014 when shoulder problems convinved him to transition to speedskating

Biographical Information 

Austin Kleba grew up in Glen View, Ill., and began ice skating at age three for ice hockey, a sport he played competitively until he was a teenager, when shoulder problems led him to switch to speedskating. Two years later, Kleba competed at the Youth Winter Olympic Games Lillehammer 2016 in speedskating, winning a silver medal in the mixed team sprint.

Six years later, Kleba would make his Olympic debut at the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022, competing in the 500m and 1,000m. Kleba won a bronze medal at the 2025 ISU World Speed Skating Single Distance Championships with teammates Cooper McLeod and Zach Stoppelmoor. The trio had previously set the world record in the event, which is not currently on the Olympic program, at an earlier ISU World Cup event in Calgary.

Off the ice, Kleba enjoys making music, something he has done since age 14. Kleba enjoys the recording aspect of making music and cites the late rapper Mac Miller as one of his main inspirations.

  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, 27th (500m - Men), 29th (1000m - Men)
World Championships Experience

  • Years of participation: Team Sprint - Men 2023, 2024, 2025; Sprint Combination - Men 2022
  • Medals: 1 (1 bronze)
    • Bronze – 2025 (Team Sprint - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Son of Barney and Janis Klecker
  • His father owns the U.S. record for the 50-mile ultramarathon (4:51:25)
  • His mother competed at the 1992 Summer Olympics (marathon) and won two U.S. marathon national championships
  • Both ran track and cross country in college
  • Klecker is one of six children
  • Three of his siblings run in college: Mary at Augsburg, John at Augustana (S.D.), and Sarah at Minnesota
  • His two other siblings, Elizabeth and James, are in high school.

  • 2019 Pac-12 Cross Country Championships, gold
  • 2018 NCAA Indoor Championships, 2nd (5,000-meter), 3rd (3,000-meter)
  • 2018 NCAA Cross Country Championships, 8th
  • 2017 Pac-12 Cross Country Championship, 2nd
  • 2017 NCAA DI Indoor Track and Field Championships, 4th (3,000-meter)
  • 2017 NCAA DI Outdoor Track and Field Championships, 7th (5,000-meter)

    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 16th (10000m - Men)
World Championships Experience

  • Years of participation: 10000m - Men 2023
  • Top finish: 20th – 2023 (10000m - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Wears No. 22
  • Parents are Mike and Brenda Kleist
  • Three words to describe her would be shy, perfectionist, and artistic/creative
  • Her biggest supporter is Mike Roberts and her family (Mom, Dad, brother and Grandma)
  • Most admires her mom because, "She's literally a superhero and I'm forever thankful for her."
  • On what it means to play for USA Softball: "A dream come true. It's been a dream since I was little and to finally be a part of it is completely surreal."
  • First memory of USA Softball/Olympics was watching Cat Osterman
  • Got started playing softball because, "My parents thought I was great at throwing a football and softball tryouts were the following week so they signed me up!"
  • Best piece of advice she has received: "Take everything a pitch at a time."
  • Her personal motto: "Kia kaha, meaning always strong and powerful."
Career Highlights
  • First Time Team USA member
